title: Arizona's Lake Hit 1% Capacity. Every Fish Died.
script: Every fish died? Arizona's San Carlos Reservoir — home to state-record largemouth bass, black crappie, flathead catfish — is now closed indefinitely. The reason: a snowpack collapse in the Gila River watershed left the reservoir less than 1% full. By late May, only 389 acre-feet of water remained in a lake that can hold far more. The fish had no chance. Wildlife officials called it a 100% fish kill — every last fish in the lake, dead. The San Carlos Recreation and Wildlife Department issued an emergency closure. Generations of prize fish, gone. What was once a popular fishing destination is now just mud. A historic lack of snow in the Gila River watershed did in weeks what climate models said would take decades. So: when does San Carlos Reservoir reopen for fishing?
